title: Schema Markup for AI
html:
Structured data (Schema.org JSON-LD) on a website that LLMs can parse cleanly when answering user prompts. Different emphasis from traditional SEO schema (which targets Google's rich-result cards) — AI-flavored schema prioritizes machine-readable facts an LLM can quote when recommending the business. Part of Rankett's standard fulfillment alongside LLM.txt and directory management. The technical side of the AEO offer.

TL;DR

  • Sits in Rankett's technical fulfillment stack: schema markup + llm.txt + directory mgmt + content updates.
  • Prioritizes quotable facts over Google's rich-card patterns — what would an LLM cite when asked "what does X company do?"
  • First named in 2026 01 07 rankett meetup prep guide as part of the AEO technical playbook.
  • Documented across the entire 2025-11 → 2026-04 productization arc — every Rankett pitch and fulfillment doc lists it.
  • Pairs with autonomous fulfillment engine — once schema implementation is templated, it's bot-deliverable at scale.

Key implementation patterns (from the vault)

  • JSON-LD blocks for entities, services, locations. 2026 02 07 simple rankett fulfillment documents the minimum viable schema set.
  • Quote-ready facts. Hours, services, named team members, awards, certifications — all LLM-citable.
  • Differentiated from rich-result schema. 2026 03 12 screenshot 2026 03 12 at 2 01 50 am 649b0022 / 2026 03 12 screenshot 2026 03 12 at 3 55 15 am 6bcc8d24 capture the "this isn't your old SEO schema" framing.
  • Vazoola-pattern competitive proof. 2026 03 06 vazoola content examples — competitors are doing manual versions; Rankett productizes.
  • Lift ATX strategy applied this. 2026 04 24 liftatx strategy uses schema-for-AI as the technical differentiator vs other gym brands.
